observed_failure.component: Backend MarketplaceWorker MCP Registry initial sync
evidence: The initial sync fetched many registry pages and issued a large batch upsert.
The batch upsert took about 36 seconds and failed with a database value-length error.
During the database pressure window, Runner readiness confirmation and heartbeat/disconnect updates timed out.
observed_failure.impact: A Runner container could complete mTLS initialization but was not reliably available to Worker creation.

repair.behavior: Development environments write and export MCP_REGISTRY_ENABLED=false. Production retains the application default.
post_restart_evidence: The restarted Backend process environment contains MCP_REGISTRY_ENABLED=false.
No new MCP Registry sync was logged after the targeted Backend restart.

revalidation.docker_queue: No active build job was present. PostgreSQL, Redis, Traefik, runner-codex-cli, and runner-e2e-echo were running.
revalidation.runner: dev-runner-codex remained online with a current tunnel and heartbeat and advertised codex-cli.
revalidation.worker_options: An authenticated ListWorkerCreateOptions request returned 200 and made only codex-cli selectable; the other 12 formal types remained explicitly blocked for missing local runtime images.
revalidation.action: The shared Docker VM contention is cleared. Resume one image build at a time and re-run the same Runner/API checks after each build.
